# Macro Monitor

> Terminal-based macro/markets dashboard for macOS: a user watchlist plus
> indexes, commodities, FX, crypto, US/Canada sovereign yield curves,
> central-bank policy rates with market-implied odds, news headlines, and an
> economic calendar. Python 3.10+, built on Textual + Rich. All data comes
> from free public APIs — no paid keys required.

## Run

- `./start.sh --live` — recommended. Sources `.env`, creates `.venv` and
  installs dependencies on first run, then starts live mode.
- `python -m macro_monitor` — static demo data. The watchlist is the
  exception: user tickers always fetch live (no demo values exist for them).
- `Macro Monitor.command` — macOS double-click launcher; equivalent to
  `./start.sh --live`.
- Flags: `--live` (real data), `--fred-key KEY` (defaults to
  `$FRED_API_KEY`), `--refresh SECONDS` (UI redraw, default 15),
  `--watchlist AAPL,NVDA` (first-run seed only), `--no-news`.
- FRED key: put `export FRED_API_KEY=...` in `./.env` (gitignored). Free at
  https://fred.stlouisfed.org. Without it, US Treasury yields show static
  fallback; everything else still goes live.

## Keyboard controls

- `q` quit
- `r` force refresh (bypasses per-endpoint TTLs)
- `c` toggle compact view
- `a` watchlist manager: type any Yahoo ticker (e.g. `AAPL`, `DOO.TO`) or a
  company name, Enter adds it (validated by a real fetch first — invalid
  symbols are rejected with an error hint). With the input empty, Enter on a
  listed row removes it. `esc` closes.
- Drag-to-swap: click-drag any of the six market tables (indexes,
  commodities, FX, crypto, UST curve, GoC curve) onto another to swap their
  positions. The order persists to `~/.config/macro_monitor/layout.txt`.
- Text selection: hold Option (⌥) while dragging from a non-swappable area —
  Textual captures the mouse.

## State and config

- Watchlist file: `~/.config/macro_monitor/watchlist.txt`, one Yahoo symbol
  per line. This file is the source of truth; edit it directly or via `a`.
- Panel layout: `~/.config/macro_monitor/layout.txt`, one panel key per line
  in slot order (4 left-column slots, then 2 right). Deleting it resets the
  arrangement; invalid contents fall back to the default order.
- `./.env`: `FRED_API_KEY`, optionally `WATCHLIST` (first-run seed).

## Architecture (read this before changing code)

- `macro_monitor/models.py` — dataclasses. `Instrument(name, value,
  change_pct, unit, category, trail, ytd_pct, dtd_pct, wtd_pct)` where
  `change_pct` is month-to-date; `trail` drives sparklines.
  `RatesInstrument` adds tenor/country. Also `PolicyRate`, `EconomicEvent`.
- `macro_monitor/data.py` — `DataProvider` ABC. `StaticDataProvider` ships
  demo numbers. `LiveDataProvider(fallback, fred_api_key, watchlist,
  live_keys)` fetches real data with three safety nets: per-endpoint TTL
  cache (prices 60s, yields 300s), a background warmer thread, and
  per-endpoint fallback to the static provider on failure. `live_keys`
  restricts which endpoints fetch live — static mode uses
  `live_keys=("watchlist",)` so only the watchlist hits the network.
  The render path only ever reads cache; it never blocks on I/O.
- `macro_monitor/watchlist.py` — `WatchlistConfig` (thread-safe file
  persistence) and `search_yahoo` (autocomplete; treat as flaky — ticker
  validation goes through the Yahoo chart → yfinance fetch path instead).
- `macro_monitor/news.py` — WSJ/FT/Bloomberg RSS polling.
- `macro_monitor/widgets.py` — pure Rich render functions; no Textual
  imports, no I/O.
- `macro_monitor/app.py` — Textual layout, timers, keybindings, watchlist
  modal. Panels re-render on a timer from cached data.

### Adding a new data feed

1. Implement `_fetch_<key>` on `LiveDataProvider` returning model objects;
   raise on failure.
2. Register it in the `_refresh` dispatcher, add a TTL to `TTLS`, and add the
   key to `LIVE_CAPABLE_KEYS` (all in `data.py`).
3. Wire `status=self.provider.status("<key>")` into the matching `DataPanel`
   in `app.py` for the source badge.

## Data sources

- Yahoo Finance chart API with yfinance fallback — indexes, FX, commodities,
  and all watchlist tickers (any Yahoo symbol incl. international, e.g.
  `DOO.TO`). Yahoo 429s trigger the yfinance fallback automatically.
- Binance klines — BTC/ETH.
- FRED (`DGS2/5/10/30`) — US Treasury curve; the one feed needing a key.
- Bank of Canada Valet — GoC curve, keyless.
- Polymarket — Fed/BoC rate-decision odds, keyless.
- Economic calendar — static demo data.

## Troubleshooting

- Watchlist row shows 0.00: first fetch is pending (populates within
  seconds) — or the symbol isn't valid on Yahoo's chart API.
- Panel badges: `● LIVE` ok · `◆ CACHED` last fetch failed, showing previous
  data · `○ FALLBACK` no fetch has ever landed, static seed shown ·
  `○ loading…` warmer hasn't finished first pass · `● STATIC` endpoint not
  live in this mode.
- Everything static + topbar says `DATA MODE: STATIC`: the app was launched
  without `--live`.
